allow:
Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
Update building group slot allocation rules

Clarify building group structure based on user input:
- Groups 1 to \(N-1\): Exactly 3 assignment slots \(required\)
- Group N \(final\): 1-3 assignment slots \(variable\)

Example: Level 5 building with 5 groups
  - Groups 1-4: 3 slots each = 12 slots
  - Group 5: 1-3 slots = 1-3 slots
  - Total: 13-15 slots

Database changes:
- Added final_group_slots column to siege_buildings table
- Added CHECK constraint \(1-3 slots\)
- Business rule documented in schema comments

UI implications:
- Groups 1 to N-1 always show 3 assignment dropdowns
- Final group shows 1-3 dropdowns based on configuration
- Total slots calculation: \(group_count - 1\) × 3 + final_group_slots

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")
Bash(python3 -c "import json,sys; m=json.load\(sys.stdin\); print\([x[''''name''''] for x in m[:5]]\)")

Bash(python3 -c "import json,sys; m=json.load\(sys.stdin\); e2e=[x for x in m if x[''''name''''].startswith\(''''E2E-''''\)]; print\(f''''E2E members: {len\(e2e\)}, active: {sum\(1 for x in e2e if x[""is_active""]\)}''''\)")
Bash(python3 -c "import json,sys; m=json.load\(sys.stdin\); print\(f''''Total: {len\(m\)}, active: {sum\(1 for x in m if x[""is_active""]\)}''''\)")

hooks.command: echo "I have these agents available: [list all available agents], i will use [name agent] to solve this task as it is the best for [reason for choosing that agent]"
